expectations.text: Identifies instanceSelector mismatch as the cause, not a generic 'check your YAML' answer
expectations.evidence: 'The dashboard doesn't appear because the instanceSelector doesn't match the Grafana CR's labels.' — directly identifies the root cause.

expectations.text: Cites the specific label dashboards: grafana as the homelab standard to match
expectations.evidence: 'The homelab standard is dashboards: grafana' and shows 'matchLabels: dashboards: grafana'.

expectations.text: Provides the exact diagnostic command: kubectl get grafana -n monitoring --show-labels
expectations.evidence: 'kubectl get grafana -n monitoring --show-labels' — exact command provided.

expectations.text: Explains that the Grafana Operator silently skips non-matching CRDs with no status error
expectations.evidence: 'The Grafana Operator silently skips CRDs whose instanceSelector doesn't match — no error is emitted to the resource status.'

expectations.text: Avoids suggesting Grafana UI edits or restarting the pod as the primary fix
expectations.evidence: Fix is entirely label-based — no UI edits or pod restarts suggested.

expectations.text: Uses label_values() syntax for the Prometheus variable and explicitly rejects it for the Loki variable
expectations.evidence: Prometheus uses 'label_values(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total, container)' and explicitly states 'Loki variables do NOT use label_values() — that's Prometheus-only.'

expectations.text: Specifies the Loki variable query as {"label": "container", "stream": "{}"} (object form, not label_values)
expectations.evidence: Loki variable query is '{ "label": "container", "stream": "{}" }' — correct object form.

expectations.evidence: Prometheus datasource uses '"uid": "${DS_PROMETHEUS}"' and Loki uses '"uid": "${DS_LOKI}"'.

expectations.text: Sets refresh: 2 on the variable definitions so they re-query on time range change
expectations.evidence: Both variable definitions include '"refresh": 2'.
